description: Complete the treb verify command to reach full feature parity with the Go CLI. Adds multi-verifier support, per-verifier shorthand flags, environment variable API key resolution, styled output using Phase 3 formatting, and golden file coverage.

userStories.title: Per-Verifier Shorthand Flags and Verifier List Resolution
userStories.description: As a developer, I want --etherscan, --blockscout, and --sourcify boolean CLI flags so that I can ergonomically select one or more verifiers without using the generic --verifier flag.
acceptanceCriteria: `treb verify <dep> --etherscan` verifies against etherscan only
`treb verify <dep> --etherscan --sourcify` verifies against both etherscan and sourcify
`treb verify <dep>` (no flags) defaults to etherscan via --verifier default
`treb verify <dep> --verifier sourcify` still works for single verifier selection
Shorthand flags override --verifier when both are specified
verify.rs::run() and run_batch() accept &[String] instead of &str for verifier parameter
Typecheck passes (`cargo check -p treb-cli`)

userStories.title: Multi-Verifier Verification Loop and Aggregate Status
userStories.description: As a developer, I want verification to run against multiple verifiers in sequence and compute an aggregate status so that I can verify a contract on all explorers in a single invocation.
acceptanceCriteria: Single deployment verification against N verifiers runs each verifier sequentially
Each verifier result recorded in deployment.verification.verifiers HashMap with lowercase key
Aggregate status: all VERIFIED -> Verified, all FAILED -> Failed, mixed -> Partial
Registry update_deployment() called once after all verifiers complete (not per-verifier)
Rate limiting delay (--delay) applied between verifier attempts
--force flag applies to all selected verifiers
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Per-Verifier API Key Resolution from Environment
userStories.description: As a developer, I want verifier API keys to resolve from standard environment variables (ETHERSCAN_API_KEY, BLOCKSCOUT_API_KEY) so that I can set keys once and verify against multiple providers without repeating them on the CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: When verifier is etherscan and no --verifier-api-key, check ETHERSCAN_API_KEY env var
When verifier is blockscout and no --verifier-api-key, check BLOCKSCOUT_API_KEY env var
When verifier is sourcify, no API key lookup needed (sourcify is keyless)
Explicit --verifier-api-key always takes precedence over env var for all verifiers
When multiple verifiers are selected, each gets its own key from its respective env var
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Styled Single Deployment Verification Output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want styled verification output with stage indicators, colored status, and verification badges so that the verify command output matches the Phase 3 formatting used by other commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Verification start shows stage indicator via print_stage()
Per-verifier results displayed with styled status: green for VERIFIED, red for FAILED
Successful verification shows explorer URL indented under verifier name
Already-verified skip message uses styled output
All styled output gated on color::is_color_enabled() using styled() pattern
Non-JSON output goes to stderr for progress, stdout for results
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Styled Batch Verification Output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want styled batch verification output with per-deployment progress counters and a summary table so that batch verification results are clear and consistent with other command output.
acceptanceCriteria: Batch progress shows print_stage() per deployment with counter: [1/5] Verifying ContractName
Per-verifier result within each deployment shows styled status inline
Summary output uses build_table() with styled Status column (green/red/yellow)
Summary table columns: Contract, Address, Status, Badge
--all --force output shows re-verifying vs verifying
Non-JSON output: progress to stderr, summary to stdout
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Multi-Verifier JSON Output Schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I want JSON output from verify to include per-verifier breakdowns so that automation tools can parse individual verifier results.
acceptanceCriteria: VerifyOutputJson gains verifiers field with per-verifier detail (status, url, reason)
Single deployment JSON: status is aggregate, verifiers contains per-verifier breakdown
Batch JSON array: each element has aggregate status + verifiers breakdown
All JSON output paths use output::print_json() for sorted keys
--json suppresses all non-JSON output (no stderr progress leaks)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Golden File Updates for Verify Command
userStories.description: As a developer, I want golden file tests covering multi-verifier scenarios, per-verifier flags, and styled output so that verify command output is regression-tested.
acceptanceCriteria: Existing golden files updated for new styled output format
New golden files added for multi-verifier flags (e.g., --etherscan --sourcify)
New golden files added for per-verifier shorthand flag usage
New golden files added for JSON output with multi-verifier results
All golden file tests pass: `cargo test -p treb-cli -- verify`
No pre-existing golden file failures introduced
Typecheck passes
